name: Unit Conversion
description: Convert a numeric value from its per-row source unit to a fixed target unit, using a scalar factor looked up in the unit_conversions vocabulary keyed by (source_unit, target_unit). Linear conversions only — non-linear conversions (e.g. temperature) require a different method.

formula.default_expression: result_value := round(value * factor(source_unit, target_unit), precision, rounding_method); result_unit := target_unit

configurations.name: target_unit
configurations.description: Target unit code. Valid values are resolved at runtime from the distinct to_code / to_name entries in model/vocabularies/unit_conversions.json, filtered by the physical dimension of the source measure (length/mass/etc.). No default — must be chosen per transformation.

configurations.name: precision
configurations.description: Number of decimal places to round result_value to after multiplication. If null/unset, no rounding is applied and the raw multiplied value is returned. For complex rounding rules (e.g. banker's rounding for lab values with parameter-specific precision), leave null and chain M.Rounding downstream.

configurations.name: rounding_method
configurations.description: Rounding convention when precision is set. Same enum as M.Rounding. Ignored when precision is null.

configurations.name: on_missing_factor
configurations.description: Behaviour when no (source_unit, target_unit) entry is found in the vocabulary. fail: raise an error. na: set result_value to NA. passthrough: keep the original value unchanged. (This is a closed set of engine control-flow modes, not a domain vocabulary — enum is intentional here.)

output_classes.description: The numeric value expressed in target_unit.
The unit code of the converted value — equal to the target_unit configuration, propagated to the output so downstream derivations/analyses can read the Measure's unit without re-reading the config.

assumptions: The (source_unit, target_unit) pair exists in the unit_conversions vocabulary for every row.
Conversion is linear: target_value = source_value * scalar_factor.
Non-linear conversions (e.g. Fahrenheit↔Celsius, affine transforms) are out of scope — use a separate method.

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: The numeric value to convert. Resolves via Observation.Result.Value facet → SDTM --ORRES (e.g. VSORRES for Height).

input_roles.name: source_unit
input_roles.description: The unit of the source value, varying per row. Resolves via Observation.Result.Unit facet → SDTM --ORRESU (e.g. VSORRESU for Height).
